title: Insilico Medicine Unveils Industry's First Longevity Board, Chaired by Eli Lilly's Andrew Adams
description: Clinical-stage generative AI biotech Insilico Medicine announced the strategic formation of the industry's first Longevity Board to accelerate AI-driven aging research. Chaired by Eli Lilly's Andrew Adams, the Board will oversee work in life models, biomarkers of aging, dual-purpose targets, and clinical development validating therapeutics against hallmarks of aging. The move formalizes longevity as a discrete investment thesis at a top AI-biotech.

title: BioAge Reports Positive Phase 1 Data for BGE-102, Advancing Obesity and Aging Therapeutic
description: BioAge Labs (Nasdaq: BIOA) reported positive Phase 1 results for BGE-102, its next-generation small molecule NLRP3 inhibitor targeting metabolic disease through the biology of human aging. The announcement marks one of the first successful clinical readouts since the company refocused after last year's azelaprag setback, restoring confidence in aging-targeted drug development.

title: New Study Challenges Rapamycin Dogma: mTOR Inhibitor May Blunt Exercise Response in Humans
description: A new human study suggests rapamycin, the most-validated longevity compound in mice, may actually blunt key benefits of exercise such as muscle protein synthesis and adaptation. This complicates dosing strategy for longevity use -- timing around exercise may matter significantly -- and echoes the sex-specific signals seen in the PEARL trial.

title: Mitrix Bio Moves Mitochondrial Transplantation From Lab to First Human Trials
description: Mitrix Bio is advancing whole-mitochondria transplantation into the clinic, targeting age-related mitochondrial decline -- a root-cause driver of aging according to the hallmarks framework. If successful, the approach could complement small-molecule mitochondrial uncouplers and NAD+ boosters with an entirely new therapeutic modality.

title: On Aging 2026 Opens in Atlanta With Peter Kaldes Keynote as 11th Global Congress Closes in Dubai
description: The On Aging 2026 opening general session featured Next50 President Peter Kaldes addressing thousands of aging professionals at the Hyatt Regency Atlanta. Meanwhile, the 11th Global Congress on Aging and Gerontology wrapped in Dubai. Together they highlighted the field's global momentum, with sessions on biomarkers, AI-driven diagnostics, and belonging as a social determinant of aging.

title: Insilico Medicine Announces Industry's First Longevity Board
description: Insilico Medicine (3696.HK) formed the industry's first Longevity Board, chaired by Andrew Adams (Group VP Molecular Discovery at Eli Lilly), to oversee AI-enabled aging research and drug discovery.

title: BioAge Reports Positive Phase 1 Data for BGE-102 NLRP3 Inhibitor
description: BioAge Labs (Nasdaq: BIOA) reported positive Phase 1 clinical trial data for BGE-102, a next-generation NLRP3 inhibitor developed for metabolic diseases by targeting the biology of human aging.

title: Rapamycin Might Blunt Exercise Response in Humans, New Study Suggests
description: A new human study indicates that rapamycin -- the most-validated lifespan extender in mice -- may reduce certain adaptive responses to exercise, with implications for timing and dosing strategies in human longevity applications.

title: Mitrix Bio Advances Mitochondrial Transplantation Into Human Clinical Trials
description: Mitrix Bio is moving mitochondrial transplantation into clinical trials, a novel therapeutic modality targeting age-related mitochondrial dysfunction as a foundational driver of aging.

title: On Aging 2026 Opening Session Features Next50 President Peter Kaldes
description: The opening general session of the nation's largest multidisciplinary aging conference featured Peter Kaldes, President and CEO of Next50, addressing thousands of professionals on April 21 at the Hyatt Regency Atlanta.

title: Kaeberlein and LaFleur Launch 700+ Person Randomized Rapamycin Trial, $38M VITAL-H Study
description: Dr. Matt Kaeberlein discusses with University of Arizona's Dr. Bonnie LaFleur the 700+ participant randomized clinical trial testing rapamycin for resilience and immune aging in adults 65+. The trial is part of the broader $38M VITAL-H federal study also evaluating semaglutide and dapagliflozin.

title: Diego Carrete: Longevity-Specific Medical Segment Projected at $31.6B in 2026
description: Investment analysis highlights the longevity economy at $27 trillion globally, with the medical segment alone projected at $31.6B in 2026 per Mordor Intelligence.

title: Pineal Gland Aging Study and Gompertz Law in Nematodes: Fight Aging Weekly Research Roundup
description: This week's research coverage includes pineal gland aging, Gompertz law in C. elegans (Nature Comms), ATF5 in muscle aging, and rhythm of aging stability and drift in individual senescence rates.

title: Homoharringtonine (HHT) Identified as Senotherapeutic That Extends Mouse Lifespan
description: Screen of 2,150 FDA-approved drugs identified HHT as a senotherapeutic that clears senescent adipocytes, restores white adipose tissue function, lowers inflammation, and extends lifespan in progeroid and naturally aged mice.

title: Barcelona Supercomputing Center Reveals Female Immune System Ages Much More Than Male
description: First-of-its-kind analysis from BSC shows the female immune system undergoes substantially greater age-related changes than the male system, with implications for sex-specific aging therapies.

title: Terbinafine and Miglustat: FDA-Approved Drugs Extend C. elegans Lifespan via UPRmt
description: Two FDA-approved drugs -- terbinafine (antifungal) and miglustat (Gaucher disease) -- extend worm lifespan by activating the mitochondrial unfolded protein response, with existing safety profiles potentially accelerating human trials.

title: Tau Seeds Induce Neurofibrillary Tangles Across Brain Regions via Individual-Specific Connectivity
description: New research establishes tau seeds spread through the brain following individual neural connectivity patterns rather than physical proximity, with implications for Alzheimer's disease progression modeling.

title: Engineered Stem Cells Become Lifelong Protein Factories Against HIV, Malaria, and Flu
description: Rockefeller researchers engineered hematopoietic stem cells to produce antibodies indefinitely. As few as 29 edited cells produced measurable levels -- platform could be adapted for any therapeutic protein.

title: A4M Longevity SpringFest 2026: Biological Age Testing to Become Routine Baseline Marker
description: 3,000+ practitioners at A4M's spring gathering identified five emerging care trends: biological age testing baseline, brainspan as new endpoint, stress as therapeutic target, personalized protocols, and informed self-directed patients.

title: Longevity Segment Investability Tracker: Cellular Reprogramming Leads With $1.28B Raised
description: Two-year tracker shows cellular reprogramming at $1.28B (6 deals), age-related disease drugs at $835M (11 deals). Reprogramming: $4.7B cumulative, producing only one Phase 1 enrollment to date.

title: ITP Reports 11 Compounds Including Astaxanthin and Alpha-Ketoglutarate Fail to Extend Mouse Lifespan
description: NIA Interventions Testing Program tested 11 compounds in UM-HET3 mice across three sites. None extended lifespan, with some reducing female lifespan -- underscoring translation difficulty.

title: USPTO Publishes AI-Driven Senolytic Compound Patent Covering 800,000+ Molecules
description: Patent application US20260097022A1 discloses screening of 2,352 compounds for senolytic activity and neural networks trained to predict activity across 800,000+ molecules targeting Bcl-2 proteins.

title: Rubedo Announces Positive Phase 1 Results for RLS-1496 Senolytic
description: Rubedo reported preliminary results from an ascending-dose trial in patients with plaque psoriasis, atopic dermatitis, and skin aging, with dose-dependent clinical signals and histologic evidence of senescent cell clearance -- first GPX4 modulator to enter human trials.

title: Life Biosciences' ER-100 Trial: Is the FDA Warming to Rejuvenation Therapies?
description: FDA cleared Life Biosciences' IND filing for ER-100 -- an epigenetic treatment for partial cellular reprogramming in the human eye -- signaling a possible regulatory route for longevity interventions.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches NIA-Funded Multi-Phase Rapamycin Clinical Trial
description: Randomized, placebo-controlled trial of approximately 84 older adults will evaluate rapamycin dosing, safety, and both short- and longer-term effects on healthy aging. A key component of the broader VITAL-H initiative.

title: Laromestrocel Stem Cell Therapy Reverses Aging-Related Frailty in Phase 2b Trial
description: Phase 2b trial in 148 adults aged 70-85 showed a single dose improved 6-minute walk test distance by 63.4 meters. About 30.8% of treated patients improved to non-frail status.

title: Daily Multivitamin Slows Biological Aging by Four Months in Harvard COSMOS Trial
description: COSMOS randomized clinical trial found daily multivitamin slowed five measures of epigenetic aging by approximately four months over two years, with greater benefits for biologically older participants.

title: Insilico Medicine and Eli Lilly Announce $2.75B AI-Driven R&D Collaboration
description: Lilly licensed assets from Insilico's AI-driven pipeline in a collaboration potentially worth up to $2.75B, signaling Big Pharma confidence in AI-driven longevity drug discovery.

title: Insilico Medicine Nominates ISM6200 as Best-in-Class NR3C1 Inhibitor
description: Insilico Medicine nominated ISM6200 as a potential best-in-class selective NR3C1 inhibitor for ovarian cancer, Cushing's syndrome, hypercortisolism-associated obesity, and glaucoma.

title: ARPA-H Commits $144M Through PROSPR Program to Fund First Large-Scale Aging Trials
description: ARPA-H awarded $144M to seven teams to build groundwork for Phase 3 trials of healthspan interventions including rapamycin, SGLT-2 inhibitors, and semaglutide in healthy older adults.
